blocks.md: A traditional function is a named multi-line function. Here it is built with `âژ•FX`, which takes character vectors as source lines. The first line is the header: result variable, function name, and arguments. The remaining lines are the body.
In a desktop APL session, traditional functions are often edited with `âˆ‡`. In this browser, `âژ•FX` is the direct way to define them.
Inside the body, `â†’label` branches. The form `â†’(condition)/label` branches only when the condition is true. `â†’0` exits the function. A function can also call itself; that is recursion.

blocks.code: âژ•FX 'Râ†گfib N' 'Râ†گN' 'â†’(N<2)/0' 'Râ†گ(fib N-1)+fib N-2' â‹„ fib 10

blocks.prompt: Use `âژ•FX` to define `dbl` with header `Râ†گdbl N`. It should set `R` to twice `N`. It will be tested as `dbl 21` and should give 42.
blocks.hint: pass the header string first, then the body string

blocks.prompt: Define recursive `fact` with `âژ•FX`. The base case should exit when `Nâ‰¤1`. It will be tested as `fact 6` and should give 720.
blocks.hint: set Râ†گ1; if Nâ‰¤1, exit; otherwise set Râ†گNأ—fact N-1

blocks.prompt: Define recursive `fib` with `âژ•FX`, where `fib 0` is 0 and `fib 1` is 1. It will be tested as `fib 12` and should give 144.
blocks.hint: start with Râ†گN; if N<2, exit; otherwise sum the two previous calls

blocks.prompt: Define dyadic `mymax` with header `Râ†گA mymax B`. Use a branch, not `âŒˆ`. It will be tested as `3 mymax 8` and should give 8.
blocks.hint: start with Râ†گA; if Aâ‰¥B, exit; otherwise set Râ†گB
